title: Kraft Group, Foxborough announce World Cup funding deal for Gillette Stadium security
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Kraft Group and Boston Soccer 2026 will fund security costs for the World Cup at Gillette Stadium.
Foxborough will not incur costs for World Cup security after reaching an agreement with Kraft Group.
Seven World Cup matches, starting with Haiti vs. Scotland, are set for Gillette Stadium this June.

title: Natick mom to run the 2026 Boston Marathon for kinder communities
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Danica Champion, a Natick mother and ESL teacher, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spark Kindness, Inc.
Spark Kindness focuses on empowering children, promoting values like kindness and empathy, and improving community health outcomes.
Champion supports the cause due to her experiences with bullying and discrimination and hopes to raise funds for Spark's programs.

title: 10 predictions for the Red Sox this season
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Garrett Crochet predicted to win the Cy Young Award after an impressive last season with an 18-5 record and 2.59 ERA.
Trevor Story expected to improve fielding performance after committing a career-high 19 errors last season.
Red Sox projected to finish 92-70, advance past the wild card series, but lose in the American League Division Series.

title: Developer vows to present more ‘options’ for Davis Square tower project amid neighborhood opposition
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Developer Copper Mill committed to presenting multiple new options for the Davis Square tower project next month.
The proposed 502-unit apartment building includes 126 affordable units and plans to use a 100% union workforce.
Community concerns include the project's size and limited two- and three-bedroom units; residents call for plans more aligned with local character.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for his 2 nephews with down syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Ponte is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for his nephews Sebastian and Zachary, who have Down syndrome.
Sebastian has passed away, and Ponte aims to honor his memory and support Zachary's future through the marathon.
The Massachusetts Down Syndrome Congress supported their family during Sebastian and Zachary's medical challenges.

title: Boston firefighter, National Guard member reportedly injured in strike that killed 6 U.S. soldiers
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Army National Guard Major Mark Garcia, a Boston firefighter, was injured in a drone strike in Kuwait that killed six U.S. soldiers.
Garcia is recovering in Germany after the Iranian drone strike; Gov. Maura Healey discussed his recovery timeline in a recent address.
Garcia serves with Engine 50 in Charlestown; the firefighters' union expressed pride and relief over his safety and recovery.

title: Lowell murder trial delayed after lead State Police investigator allegedly causes fatal crash while driving drunk
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The retrial for the Phan brothers, accused of a 2020 Lowell murder, is delayed indefinitely due to a legal issue with the lead investigator.
State Police Sgt. Scott Quigley, who investigated the murder case, was allegedly involved in a fatal crash while driving drunk in December 2023.
Justice Christopher Barry-Smith cited the ongoing investigation into Quigley as a reason for delaying the trial set for April 27.

title: ​Bruins’ playoff hopes might rest on Jeremy Swayman’s shoulders for this stretch run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman has a .906 save percentage this season, an improvement from last year's .892.
Swayman's performance has been crucial during the Bruins' 13-game home win streak, achieving a .939 save percentage in nine games.
The Bruins hold the second Wild Card spot in the Eastern Conference, one point behind the Red Wings.

title: Watch: Body cam shows Patriots party at downtown Boston restaurant
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Body camera footage shows Patriots' private party at Estella restaurant with hookahs, strippers, and after-hours drinking.
Boston police responded to Estella on Jan. 27, finding smoking violations and unauthorized adult entertainment at the party.
The restaurant owner received a three-day license suspension, blaming Patriots players for unpaid tabs and disorderliness.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots safety Kevin Byard
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots signed safety Kevin Byard to a one-year, $9 million deal, adding an experienced leader to their defense.
Byard led the NFL with seven interceptions in 2025 and was part of the Bears' league-best plus-22 turnover differential.
Byard, who has never missed an NFL game, has three First Team All-Pro nods and 36 career interceptions.
